question_text: A spring with spring constant 200 N/m is compressed by 0.1 m and then released to launch a 0.5 kg ball horizontally. Calculate the elastic potential energy released by the spring.
explanation: Elastic potential energy is calculated using E_e = ½kx², where k = 200 N/m and x = 0.1 m. So E_e = ½ × 200 × (0.1)² = ½ × 200 × 0.01 = 1 J. This energy will be transferred to the ball as kinetic energy when the spring is released.
